query: SocioMetrica GAD3 Invymark 40dB Spain polls PP lead June 2026 latest fieldwork
answer:
# Spanish Opinion Polling: PP Lead – SocioMetrica, GAD3, Invymark, 40dB (Latest Fieldwork, Late May / Early June 2026)

## Overview

Data was extracted from the Wikipedia article on opinion polling for the next Spanish general election, accessed on June 18, 2026. **None of the four target pollsters have fieldwork entirely within June 2026 yet**; the most recent data from these firms covers late May into early June. The most recent purely-June fieldwork comes from other firms (EM-Analytics, Sigma Dos, CIS).

---

## Poll-by-Poll Findings

### 1. SocioMetrica (for El Español) — Fieldwork: 28–30 May 2026 (n=1,200)
- **PP: 33.9%** (147 seats projected) | **PSOE: 25.9%** (105 seats) | **PP Lead: 8.0 pp**
- Vox: 17.4% (60 seats) | Sumar: 5.7% (6 seats) | Podemos: 2.2% | SALF: 2.5%

### 2. GAD3 (for ABC) — Fieldwork: 26–28 May 2026 (n=1,011)
- **PP: 33.1%** (141 seats projected) | **PSOE: 26.8%** (107 seats) | **PP Lead: 6.3 pp**
- Vox: 17.1% (60 seats) | Sumar: 5.9% (7 seats) | Podemos: 3.1% | SALF: 1.4%

### 3. Invymark (for laSexta) — Fieldwork: 25–29 May 2026 (n=1,200)
- **PP: 33.1%** | **PSOE: 27.8%** | **PP Lead: 5.3 pp**
- Vox: 17.6% | Sumar: 5.5% | Podemos: 3.6% | SALF: 1.7%

### 4. 40dB (for Prisa / El País) — Fieldwork: 28 May – 1 June 2026 (n=2,000)
- **PP: 32.4%** | **PSOE: 27.7%** | **PP Lead: 4.7 pp**
- Vox: 17.4% | Sumar: 6.2% | Podemos: 2.3% | SALF: 2.1%

---

## Summary Comparison

| Pollster | Commissioner | Fieldwork | n | PP | PSOE | PP Lead | Vox | Sumar |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SocioMetrica | El Español | 28–30 May | 1,200 | 33.9% | 25.9% | **8.0 pp** | 17.4% | 5.7% |
| GAD3 | ABC | 26–28 May | 1,011 | 33.1% | 26.8% | **6.3 pp** | 17.1% | 5.9% |
| Invymark | laSexta | 25–29 May | 1,200 | 33.1% | 27.8% | **5.3 pp** | 17.6% | 5.5% |
| 40dB | Prisa | 28 May–1 Jun | 2,000 | 32.4% | 27.7% | **4.7 pp** | 17.4% | 6.2% |

**Average PP lead across all four: ~6.1 percentage points**

---

## Key Observations

1. **PP leads PSOE in all four polls**, with the lead ranging from **4.7 pp (40dB) to 8.0 pp (SocioMetrica)**.
2. **PP vote share** clusters between 32.4%–33.9% (average ~33.1%).
3. **PSOE vote share** ranges from 25.9%–27.8% (average ~27.1%).
4. **Vox** is remarkably consistent at **17.1%–17.6%**, a significant increase from 2025 levels (~10–12%).
5. **Sumar** has declined sharply to 5.5%–6.2%, down from ~11–12% in late 2023/early 2024.
6. **House effects** are visible: SocioMetrica (El Español) shows the widest PP lead; 40dB (Prisa/El País) shows the narrowest—a well-known pattern in Spanish polling.
7. **Seat projections** (where available) suggest PP+Vox would command ~201–207 seats, well above the 176-seat absolute majority threshold.
8. **SALF (Se Acabó La Fiesta)**, Alvise Pérez's party, polls at 1.4%–2.5% across the four firms.

sources.title: Opinion polling for the next Spanish general election – Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Comprehensive table of Spanish national opinion polls including SocioMetrica, GAD3, Invymark, and 40dB with fieldwork dates, vote shares, and seat projections.

key_facts: PP leads PSOE across all four target pollsters (SocioMetrica, GAD3, Invymark, 40dB), with leads ranging from 4.7 to 8.0 percentage points as of late May / early June 2026 fieldwork.
Average PP lead across the four pollsters is approximately 6.1 percentage points.
PP vote share averages ~33.1% (range 32.4%–33.9%); PSOE averages ~27.1% (range 25.9%–27.8%).
Vox is highly consistent across all four polls at 17.1%–17.6%, representing a near-doubling from 2025 levels.
Sumar has declined sharply to 5.5%–6.2%, down from ~11–12% in late 2023/early 2024.
40dB (fieldwork 28 May–1 Jun) is the only one of the four pollsters with fieldwork extending into June 2026; the others have late-May fieldwork only.
Seat projections from SocioMetrica and GAD3 suggest PP+Vox coalition would reach 201–207 seats, well above the 176-seat majority threshold.
Known house effects: SocioMetrica (El Español) consistently shows the widest PP-PSOE gap; 40dB (Prisa/El País) consistently shows the narrowest.
SALF (Se Acabó La Fiesta / Alvise Pérez) polls between 1.4% and 2.5% across the four firms.
Podemos ranges from 2.2% to 3.6%, remaining a minor force.
